persons.arguments_against: The strongest counter-hypothesis is a same-name relative: Abayomi-Cole is a known Sierra Leone Creole family name, and a father, brother, or cousin with the same 'A. B.' initials could have served as a Medical Officer in the same colony. The deterministic edition_cooccurrence check also fails (stint in 1949-1951 editions, biography only in 1956-1963 editions), which could suggest two different people whose listings never coincide. This fails for several reasons. First, the corpus context shows exactly 1 official and 1 biography with this surname across the entire graph — there is no second Abayomi-Cole anywhere in the staff lists or biographical sections to be confused with. Second, the biography's own career clause states he was appointed 'M.O., S.L., 1944' and was not promoted to S.M.O. until 1955, which means the biography itself asserts he was serving as a Medical Officer in Sierra Leone throughout 1949-1951 — exactly what the stint records. The position_sim FAIL is a pure abbreviation artifact ('M.O.' vs 'Medical Officer'); read as text the positions are identical. Third, the edition gap is explained mechanically: the biography simply does not appear in editions before 1956, so no co-occurrence was possible; the stint window (1949-1951) sits squarely inside the bio's attested M.O. tenure (1944-1955). Fourth, this is a two-initial match ('A. B.'), not the single-initial trap, and the age gate passes comfortably (age 42 at stint start, mid-career, consistent with a 1944 appointment). No realistic counter-hypothesis survives.
persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second A. B. Abayomi-Cole in Sierra Leone government service in the late 1940s (e.g., a relative in the Medical Department staff lists), or evidence that the biography's subject was outside Sierra Leone or not yet in the medical service during 1949-1951 (e.g., a record placing him at Durham University or in another colony in those years).

notes: Clean single-bio, single-stint case with a rare hyphenated surname (unique in corpus). The two deterministic FAILs are artifacts: position_sim fails only on abbreviation ('M.O.' vs 'Medical Officer'), and edition_cooccurrence fails because the biography first appears in 1956, after the stint's edition window — the bio's stated 1944-1955 M.O. tenure fully covers the 1949-1951 stint. Honour FAIL is expected: the O.B.E. was awarded in 1960, after the stint years.
